name: angular-component
description: Create modern Angular standalone components following v20+ best practices. Use for building UI components with signal-based inputs/outputs, OnPush change detection, host bindings, content projection, and lifecycle hooks. Triggers on component creation, refactoring class-based inputs to signals, adding host bindings, or implementing accessible interactive components.
capabilities: Create standalone Angular components with signal-based inputs and outputs
Implement OnPush change detection strategy for optimal performance
Use host bindings for component metadata without decorators
Build accessible components with ARIA attributes and keyboard support
Implement content projection with multi-slot support
author: analogjs
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate Angular v20+ component documentation with signal-based inputs, outputs, host bindings, content projection, and lifecycle hooks. The 91 flagged patterns are markdown code fences for TypeScript examples, not actual shell commands or security vulnerabilities.
valueStatement: Create high-performance Angular components with signal-based reactivity, OnPush change detection, and modern v20 patterns for optimal rendering performance.
limitations: Does not generate complete application scaffolding
Does not provide state management solutions like NgRx
Requires Angular v17+ for signal-based APIs

useCases.title: Build reusable UI component library
useCases.description: Create a set of consistent, well-typed components following Angular best practices for use across multiple projects

useCases.title: Refactor legacy components to signals
useCases.description: Convert class-based @Input/@Output to modern signal-based inputs/outputs for better performance

useCases.title: Implement accessible interactive components
useCases.description: Build form controls and interactive elements with proper ARIA support and keyboard navigation

promptTemplates.title: Create basic component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Angular standalone component called [ComponentName] with required input [inputName] of type [Type], optional input [optInput] with default value [default], and an output for [eventName].
promptTemplates.scenario: Generating a simple component with inputs and outputs

promptTemplates.title: Build form control component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Angular component with two-way binding using model() for [value]. Include validation, error messages, and accessibility attributes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building reusable form control with validation

promptTemplates.title: Implement complex content projection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a card component with header, body, and footer content projection slots. Use ng-content with selectors for each slot.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating components with multiple projection areas

promptTemplates.title: Build lazy-loaded feature component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a dashboard component that uses @defer to lazy-load a heavy chart component when it enters the viewport.

bestPractices: Use signal-based inputs and outputs instead of class-based @Input/@Output decorators
Always use OnPush change detection strategy for optimal performance
Use host object instead of @HostBinding and @HostListener decorators
Implement accessibility with proper ARIA attributes and keyboard support
antiPatterns: Do NOT use standalone: true in Angular v20+ (components are standalone by default)
Do NOT use ngClass or ngStyle (use direct class and style bindings)
Do NOT use constructor injection (use inject() function instead)

faq.question: What Angular version is required for this skill?
faq.answer: Angular v17 or later is required for signal-based APIs. Angular v20+ is recommended for the latest patterns.

faq.question: Are the components SSR-compatible?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes afterNextRender and afterRender for SSR-safe DOM manipulation.

faq.question: Can I use RxJS with these components?
faq.answer: Yes, use outputFromObservable for RxJS interop with signal outputs.

faq.question: How do I handle two-way binding?
faq.answer: Use the model() function which creates both input and output for [(value)] syntax.

faq.question: Do components support lazy loading?
faq.answer: Yes, use @defer blocks with triggers like on viewport, on idle, on interaction.

faq.question: How do I query elements in the template?
faq.answer: Use viewChild and viewChildren for querying elements and components in the template.
